name: Collado del Lobo — circular desde Serradilla
region_intro: Hiking around Serradilla offers access to the diverse landscapes of Monfragüe National Park, a protected area characterized by Mediterranean forest, scrubland, dramatic river gorges, and quartzite ridges. The region features a network of trails that traverse varied terrain, including ancient geological formations and areas rich in biodiversity. These natural features provide a range of hiking experiences within a compact geographical area. Best hiking trails around Serradilla The most popular hiking route is Cerro Gimio - Monfragüe National Park – circular via the Green Route from Villarreal de San Carlos, a 4.9 miles (7.8 km) trail that takes 2 hours 21 minutes to complete. This moderate circular path explores the Monfragüe National Park, passing the Cerro Gimio peak. Another top favourite among local hikers is Friar's Gorge Trail, a moderate 6.2 miles (9.9 km) path. This trail navigates through a notable gorge, offering views of the surrounding natural rock formations and vegetation. Local hikers also love the Monfragüe Castle Trail (Red Route), a 4.8 miles (7.8 km) trail leading through Mediterranean forest and towards the historic Monfragüe Castle, often completed in about 2 hours 41 minutes. Hiking around Serradilla is defined by its Mediterranean forests, dramatic river gorges, and prominent quartzite ridges. The network of trails offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult ability levels. The routes in Serradilla are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 200 reviews. More than 1200 hikers have used komoot to explore Serradilla's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Serradilla?
A: Serradilla offers access to over 18 hiking routes, providing a variety of experiences within the Monfragüe National Park. These trails are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes in Serradilla?
A: The terrain around Serradilla is characterized by the diverse landscapes of Monfragüe National Park. You'll encounter Mediterranean forest, scrubland, dramatic river gorges carved by the Tajo and Tiétar Rivers, and prominent quartzite ridges. Trails often feature ancient geological formations and areas rich in biodiversity.

Q: What are the difficulty levels of hiking trails in Serradilla?
A: The trails around Serradilla cater to various fitness levels. While the majority, 15 out of 18, are considered moderate, there are also 2 easier routes and 1 more difficult option available. This ensures there's a suitable path for most hikers.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Serradilla?
A: Yes, there are several circular routes available. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990441671/cerro-gimio-monfraguee-national-park-circular-via-the-green-route-from-villarreal-de-san-carlos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro Gimio - Monfragüe National Park – circular via the Green Route from Villarreal de San Carlos&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 7.8 km path exploring the national park and passing the Cerro Gimio peak. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1196694917/collado-del-lobo-circular-desde-serradilla" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collado del Lobo — circular desde Serradilla&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 13.6 km route.

Q: What natural landmarks or viewpoints can I see while hiking in Serradilla?
A: Hiking in Serradilla offers access to spectacular natural landmarks and viewpoints within Monfragüe National Park. You can visit the iconic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5679306" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Gypsy's Leap&lt;/a&gt; (Salto del Gitano), offering dramatic gorge views, or explore &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/429392" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monfragüe Castle&lt;/a&gt; for panoramic clifftop vistas. Other notable spots include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5323985" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Serrano Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/808762" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View from Cerro Gimio&lt;/a&gt;. The confluence of the Tagus and Tiétar rivers is also a significant natural feature.

Q: Is Serradilla a good place for birdwatching or wildlife spotting?
A: Absolutely. Serradilla's location within Monfragüe National Park makes it a prime destination for birdwatching. The park is renowned for its large population of griffon vultures, eagles, and black storks, which can often be observed soaring above the dramatic cliffs. The diverse Mediterranean forest also provides habitat for various wildlife, including red and roe deer.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking options in Serradilla?
A: While many trails are moderate, the Green Route, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990441671/cerro-gimio-monfraguee-national-park-circular-via-the-green-route-from-villarreal-de-san-carlos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro Gimio - Monfragüe National Park circular&lt;/a&gt;, is generally considered an easier option suitable for families. It's a circular route of just over 7 km, starting from Villarreal de San Carlos. Always check the specific route details for elevation and terrain to ensure it matches your family's capabilities.

Q: Can I hike with my dog in Monfragüe National Park near Serradilla?
A: Monfragüe National Park has specific regulations regarding pets to protect its rich biodiversity. Generally, dogs are permitted on designated trails but must be kept on a leash at all times. It's advisable to check the latest park regulations before your visit, especially concerning access to certain protected areas or viewpoints.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Serradilla?
A: The best seasons for hiking in Serradilla and Monfragüe National Park are typically spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November). During these times, temperatures are milder, and the landscape is vibrant. Summer can be very hot, making early morning or late afternoon hikes preferable, while winter offers cooler temperatures but can be damp.

Q: Are there facilities like cafes or accommodation in Serradilla?
A: Serradilla, including the nearby Villarreal de San Carlos, serves as a convenient base for exploring the park. The village offers basic services such as bars, cafeterias, and ATMs. For accommodation, you'll find various options in and around Serradilla and Villarreal de San Carlos, ranging from guesthouses to rural hotels.

Q: How can I get to the trailheads, and is parking available?
A: Many trailheads, particularly those within Monfragüe National Park, are accessible by car. Villarreal de San Carlos, a small village within the park and close to Serradilla, is a common starting point for several routes and typically has parking facilities. Public transport options to the park are limited, so driving is often the most convenient way to reach specific trailheads.

Q: Do I need a permit to hike in Monfragüe National Park?
A: For general hiking on marked trails within Monfragüe National Park, a specific permit is usually not required. However, certain restricted areas, guided tours, or activities might require prior authorization or booking. It's always a good idea to consult the official Monfragüe National Park website or visitor center for the most current information on access and regulations.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails around Serradilla?
A: The hiking trails around Serradilla are highly regarded by the komoot community, boasting an impressive average rating of 4.7 stars from over 200 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the stunning natural beauty, the rich opportunities for birdwatching, and the well-maintained paths that lead through diverse Mediterranean landscapes and to historic sites like Monfragüe Castle.
tips: From the Collado del Lobo there are very good views of the Monfragüe park. The wolf monument has a sign that explains the legend in the local language, Serradillano.

Serradilla is a small town, a good starting point for various trails in the surrounding area, such as the Camino Natural de Tajo and the brown route in the Monfragüe National Park. It has some services such as bars, cafeterias, ATMs.
